title: Powerhouse packages are published to NPM with @org-ph scope convention and installed into any environment via ph install
content: A Powerhouse package bundles document models, editors, processors, and scripts. The NPM org naming convention is `@yourorganization-ph` (the `-ph` suffix identifies Powerhouse ecosystem packages). Workflow: `ph init` creates project, `ph vetra --interactive --watch` for development, `pnpm build` to verify, `pnpm version patch|minor|major` for semver, `pnpm publish --access public` to NPM. Installation: `ph install @org-ph/package-name` makes document models and editors available. The `powerhouse.manifest.json` file declares all modules (documentModels, editors, apps, subgraphs, importScripts). `ph install` also automatically adds package styles to `styles.css`.
description: Organizations use @org-ph NPM scope; ph install adds document models, editors, and processors to an instance.
